title: Easily worth $150, but actually affordable
text:
TLDR: After owning a $150 FitBit and a $35 LETSFIT, this tracker blows both out of the water. It's not just another Chinese knockoff. Do yourself a favor and purchase this if you want a simple watch/steps/sleep/HR/messages tracker.

In 2018 I bought a FitBit off of Craigslist. The band broke twice in 1 year and there were software bugs sprinkled throughout my time of owning it. I just didn't like how it fit. Then in Nov. 2019 I bought a LETSFIT tracker that the screen cracked on within 2 months, had really inaccurate steps reporting, and said my heart rate was almost 200 during workouts (yea right!).

So after hours of scouring Amazon for a better & more affordable tracker, 9 pages in my search for "Fitness tracker" I stumbled across this and the reviews looked great and the look of the watch looked incredible. After owning it for a week I can flat out say that this is the best tracker I've owned and possibly the best one on the market for someone who just wan

title: Not the most accurate at tracking fitness but still a great smart device
text: I’m a Xiaomi fan so I may be a little lenient and/or less harsh on my opinions of this device. I’ve never had any sort of fitness tracker/smart watch before but this, for the price seems like the perfect entry level device. I’m disappointed in the accuracy sometimes while other times it seems fine. I wouldn’t put much faith in this watch as a fitness tracker as you might want to, but as a watch that can give you notifications, weather, change your music or so on it is great. I used to hate the idea of having to charge a watch every night but even with it scanning every minute (the most frequent setting) and it vibrating a lot from my notifications I only have to charge once a week. On top of that I can take a 30 minute to an hour nap and it’ll fully charge in that time. I should mention I’ve tried this using an iPhone 7 on iOS 14 as well as a Google Pixel XL running android 10. It works fine on both but I believe there’s some features that were not on iOS. I may update in the future on

title: UPDATED: Inaccurate pedometer, distance, sleep and Heart rate
text:
I've had a MI Band 2 and decided to upgrade to the 5. The color screen is nice and the apps are decent. Battery life is excellent.

However the fitness counters are faulty. I have this paired with my Xiaomi MI 9 phone, and tested issues I was having on my wife's Redmi Note 9 (Also a Xiaomi product). No compatibility issues, but we've tried to change any available settings to improve the step counter, distance measurements, and HR monitor, but nothing works. I've tried wearing it on either arms, my wife has, as well. It consistently under counts all walking/running statistics by as much as 2 miles and several thousand steps on a 6 mile walk. It also says that our heart rate is 130-140, but physically measured it to be 80-90. I have walked with and without our phone(s) , while paired with the Band, same inaccurate results, although connected via bluetooth to the phone.

Additionally, the sleep monitor is not accurate. As an example, recently it completely missed a 10

title: Awesome, excellent value for the price!
text: Great smart band, the product is well designed and functional. I've been using it primarily to track my sleep and it's pretty accurate. The software is great. Totally worth the price.

title: It works fine and it relatively cheap
text:
I paired this with my iPhone, my S7 and my MI 10 and I like it in general.
You can for example use it to take pictures via bluetooth paired with your phone
You can also get calls notification, emails, MS teams chat messages, Telegram messages also when properly paired with your phone
It uses your cellphone GPS to track your outdoor workouts. I already tested this with my bicycle and it measures not only the distance but the heath rate, calories etc.

title: Best little activity tracker you can buy for the $$$
text:
It is a great piece of tracking equipment, I wear a nice watch, and on my other arm I wear the Band 5. Just a black nice looking tracker. I also does my sleep, steps and cost less than a new band for the Iwatch.
The screen is color, and has more features than I will ever need.

title: Lots of HUGE PROS. 1 MAMMOTH CON. Deal breaker
text:
SO MANY PROS ITS CRAZY......... But the 1 con makes it no good for a fitness tracker....read below. It’s long but very comprehensive

Lots of great features but one major flaw. I wanted this to work so bad. I read up on this this was the one I wanted. If you look at my picture it’s a Fitbit charge three. For all of you don’t know Fitbit charge three and four the back lighting is so bad that I cannot be seen during a day. It’s also bad at night. But when I said can’t be seen during the day it’s impossible. Now let’s go down the list of pros for this watch because there’s a lot more of them than there are cons believe it or not but the one con is the most important thing to me so it had to go back.
PROS:
Extremely bright. It’s level three was already twice as bright as the highest level of the Fitbit charge. Once you put it up the five it was ridiculous how it was. So bright
Because I wore the Fitbit on one arm and wore this watch and the other I got to see the a

title: Trips to the bathroom? 900 steps.
text: That would be correct if I lived in a castle but I don't. My place is small. No matter what wrist I wore the band I'd always get insane step counts. I'm pretty bummed.

title: Sleep monitoring horrible!  If U return, don't expect full refund!
text: I bought this b/c it was waterproof and I got my fitbit wet and it wasn't tracking my sleep properly.  Most importantly...it did NOT track REM.  It also gave very LITTLE details about your sleep.  No sleep score, resting heart rate, restlessness at night...nothing!  I got this on Amazon prime, actually 1 day free shipping.  The next day after I order it, they came out with the Amazfit 5 band with the Oxygen monitoring and Alexa built in for the same price available to pre-order.  I had not received my band yet.  I received it in the evening and had to charge it up.  The next morning I was able to check the sleep to see how horrible the monitoring was and was sooo mad.  I went directly to Amazon to pre-order the Amazfit and it was no longer available for pre-order.  I spent 2 hours out of my busy day via chat with Amazon trying to figure out why my band wasn't working properly, they never tried to troubleshoot, just said send it back and weren't able to help me get the pre-order.  Now I

title: LOVE THIS WATCH
text: After my Fitbit Versa died, I wasn’t sure what kind of watch to get. While debating it, I bough a cheap watch on Amazon that someone had raved about in comparison to my previous watch. It was about $35 so I got it to use until I made a decision. It was garbage. Strap broke twice, the format and display were awful. I went through 2 of them only because they replaced the first one. I’m still undecided on a sports watch so looked for a different cheap alternative, that’s when I found this. I’m literally no longer debating a Garmin or Galaxy or Apple Watch. Love this thing! Steps and HR are right on the front like my Fitbit. You can easily move things around to fit how you want to access them both on the watch and on the app. Treadmill runs it allows you to adjust the mileage at the end in case it over counts, which it has a couple times. Logs your sleep and other health things. I can’t say enough good things about it! If I had to pick a flaw, I don’t love the band closure but that’s just

title: Sleep tracking is complete garbage
text: Junk. Bought for sleep tracking and it tracks nothing or misses large chunks of time. If you go to activities and select sleep it sends an error message. No other way to indicate when you are going to sleep and it's useless at figuring it out on its own. Tried it on the inside and outside of both wrists and it's uncomfortable at best.

title: Meh App, Solid Device
text:
You have to download the Mi Fit app to even turn on this device on, and there are a few quirks that come with using it. For example, there is no option to see the weather in Fahrenheit, so you have to read the temperature in Celsius. The sleep tracking is pretty solid, but if you go to bed super late it will just assume that you skipped sleep that day. The cutoff seems to be around four or five in the morning, so it's not an issue most of the time. The pedometer has been accurate as far as I can tell. The heart rate tracking also seems fairly accurate, but of course it's limited in the same way that all watch-based heart rate monitoring is.

The watch is most comfortable, through there is a raised bump on the back of the watch where the heart rate sensor is, and that bump can kind of dig into your wrist if you wear the watch too tight. Besides that, of course the rubber/silicon watch band will leave a pattern on your wrist, and it will remain wet under the band for a pretty l

title: Inconsistent notifications, poor clasp.
text: Well only had it two weeks. Came undone randomly three times, the last time I didn’t realize it while playing disc golf so it’s lost. Does not stay secured. And while I did have it, the notifications rarely worked if at all for texts and phone calls. Very frustrating.

title: Much Love for the Global version, there's no need for a more pricey USA version
text: Mi Smart Band 5 is not officially released in the USA and it cannot show the Dollar sign $. Instead it shows the Chinese Y symbol.  Contacted Xiaomi and they told me tough luck and to bug off they don't support the Global version in USA. Well, last I heard, the USA is part of the Global community. So good luck to them ever selling me anything else.  Having said that you understand the reasoning for 3 stars and not 5. The iPhone App is not totally intuitive, but once you go through it a few times it's fine.  I'm sure when they do begin to sell the Mi Smart Band officially in USA it will be much higher priced than the imported Global version. I whole heartedly recommend the Global version to my fellow US citizens. Turns out there is no need to pay more for a USA version. I can be happy with the Y symbol instead of a dollar sign in the few messages that need it. It's not that big of a deal and saved bucks. Bought this for my wife too. Much love for the less expensive Global version, thank

title: Can recommend
text: Stylish, good software. I really like it.

title: Not Even Close to Accurate
text:
Everything appears to work fine with the Mi Fit. It can be taken in a pool, it connects well to the app, it can track sleep, it has great battery life, the screen can be seen in daylight, there is a good amount of data. It has pretty much all of the features I wanted. But none of that matters when the HR monitor is ridiculously inaccurate. The pedometer also is inaccurate but it's at least a bit closer and less important.

The HR monitor would jump from reading 47 to 98 within a minute when my position didn't change. Even at peak exercise, it read my HR as so low that it didn't think I was putting in any effort. But, I still assumed it must be close enough until I had to go to the ER for what turned out to be AFib. I was connected to a heart monitor and my Mi Fit would be reading me at 50HR when I was actually at 135. And it never really got any closer to accurate. When I was released to go home, I used my wife's Amazon Halo band so we could track my heart rate. It read it ve

title: Great product
text:
I have been using this product for last 1 month every single day. It is in my wrist about 8hours every day. I am rating the watch based on the following factors:
* Mobile App : * 1. Mobile App is decent and supports several Watch face options. There are also additional apps that you can download which makes it easier to download several 100 watch faces to Mi app and synchronize in just few seconds.
* Watch Faces: * There are about 10 to 20 watch faces available in the Mi mobile app. But through the additional free mobile app that you can download for Android, I have so far used 50+ watch faces , all of them look wonderful. I am satisfied with the options available to change the Watch faces.
* Battery Backup *: It comes for 3days for me for an average use with about 10+ notifications each day.
* Bluetooth connectivity: * Notifications are very reliable. You can configure any particular app to receive the notification alert (through vibration only, no sound alert)

title: Almost a good buy
text: I did like several things about this tracker.  Nice size, comfortable, worked well, EXCEPT for the weather function. I could never get that to sync correctly and stay synced.  I tried all the fixes proposed online, but nothing stuck.  I like to know the actual temperature -- not just a range of possible temps for the day. (I did return the tracker.)

title: Not for people who work at night
text: I like this band very much considering how cheap it is. The only thing not working for me is sleep tracking. I work the graveyard shift and sleep during the day. My sleep is always registered as 'nap' because the software only registers sleep if you sleep between 8pm and 5am... Please give us the option to change this in the app...

title: not too shabby
text: I love this cheap thing. It works well for the price. It has music but I still havent figure out how to use it. The only thing is that the weather is not of US measurement and I cant seem to figure out how to change that. The app is very simple but lacking setting options. I would still buy it becuase it measures walking steps well. I am not that active so this works for me. It has heart rate and sleep as well.

title: Doesn't work unless arm is swinging by your side.
text: Does not work when holding anything, including while pushing a stroller, holding a grocery bag, or holding a someone's hand. I purchased this to use while walking around with my family and unless my hand is swinging by my side, it doesn't register any steps. After 3 hours at a zoo with my son I was only at 900 steps due to pushing a stroller or holding his hand most of the time. Very sad that it's basically useless unless for everyday use.

title: Couldn't Even Get It To Work
text: In order to even use it, it urges you to "pair first". Okay, cool. I download the app, try to have it send the verification phone to my phone via text. I never get it. Resend in 60 seconds. 5 attempts later, I get on the website to create my account, and do so via email verification. I click "activate account" in my email, and get "this webpage is not working" , many attempts later, I finally give up and will be returning it. Cheap chinese trash. I'd avoid.

title: So many choices for face screen, Great tracker, but beware of the extra bands that are sold separate
text: I love that you don't have to take anything apart to charge it, the magnetic charger is great. They give you so many options for changing the picture on the face of the tracker. This tracker seems like it would be a really good one, it did track my steps pretty accurately and also check my heart rate regularly which was awesome. The battery life seems great, I only had it for 3 days before I lost it ☹️ thanks to the crap extra bands I purchased separately.  The band that came with this tracker stayed secured on my wrist, however I decided to change it to a brightly colored band which was obviously of lesser quality and now my tracker has disappeared. There is even a find my band thing on the app but I had my bluetooth turned off so it doesn't work 🤦. Please learn from my mistakes, enjoy this awesome band how it comes and keep your Bluetooth on just in case 😉. Magnetic charger and crap bands pictured.

title: Awesome! Arrived a week early
text: Love it! What I don't like is if I do an indoor walking exercise, it will not track it. I did one of Leslie Samsone's walk videos yesterday, and because I didn't leave my house, it wouldn't record my exercise. I have no record of it in my workouts. It did count my steps but not the activity. I will use Treadmill or Freestyle to see if it records my workout next time.

title: Mediocre HR accuracy and steps counter, but good on a budget.
text:
Pros:
1. Battery life is king. This thing conserves power well. That said, it reports that power is low when it's not on one of its screens (not the home screen), so that's pretty confusing.
2. Screen is small but vibrant and feels responsive enough for easy input.
3. Bluetooth to your phone won't kill your phone battery... it barely uses the bluetooth connection, though.
4. Heart rate BPM is fairly accurate for a watch form factor. Works well.
5. The steps counter is presented well and gives you an "amount left until goal" objective to work toward. You have some control over the goal via the mobile app.
6. Stats are stored via the mobile app and seem pretty convenient. Not sure if you can export the data, but I'll look into that.

Cons:
1. Battery indicator on home screen seems accurate, but another screen will contradict it.
2. App leaves a lot to be desired. Many features simply seem to be missing, like a tab for alarm management or

title: Always runs away
text:
The product works fine especially for price.

The band is so comfortable you won't even notice as it slips randomly from your wrist. Always waiting for the opportune moment it runs away when you least expect leaving you searching the floor for where that pesky watch went. So you use a different band only to find it still has that pesky habit of running away. I have a dozen bands, and they all cannot keep that watch on my perfectly normal sized wrist. I am not chopping down trees in the forest or going on kung fu fighting adventures. it is perfectly complacent to rest on my wrist if I'm doing a HIIT workout. It waits, and when you least expect it, it ceases the moment and flees.

I've had it less than a month and it is gone. I haven't left the house, but somehow my watch has escaped it. In the distraction of making dinner it ran away.

title: The watch needs a subscription to work all function
text: Nice watch but without the buyer getting their subscription, many functions will not work.  So be aware if you purchase this watch.  Otherwise it's ok.

title: Great smartwatch worth far more than you'll pay
text: I previously had a Fitbit Alta. This has way more features for way less cost. The charge lasts at least 5-6 days and that's with continuous heart rate monitoring on. The app isn't the fanciest around, but allows you to get what needs to be done. It's not good at tracking movement if your arm isn't moving. Walked around with hands in pocket to keep warm and it didn't track steps. Otherwise it is comfortable, not too large on the wrist, and works great.

title: Band doesn't stay on
text: Wrist band was difficult to put on and didn't stay secure.  Can't give an accurate rating of the smart wristband because it was lost the 1st week my 15 yr owned it.  He wouldn't wear it outside the house because it would fall off and he was afraid he'd lose it.  He was going out sledding and wristband was on counter and I asked why he wasn't wearing it and he said he was afraid of losing it.  I helped him put it on and sent him out. He came in later and said it fell off and he lost it!  What good is a fitness tracker if you can't wear it when active!?!? I've contacted seller and they said contact amazon for a product exchange.  When amazon was contacted they said it's not covered under warranty because the product has to be returned.  I can't return the defective product because it was LOST!  I read lots of reviews and thought this was a good product and perhaps we just got a defective band but not sure it's worth the risk of buying if company doesn't stand behind it's product.  Very d

title: App is impossible to setup
text:
App is impossible to setup, no instructions with item. Had to send it back. Useless!

The I am not a robot test had tiny images, impossible to select on a phone, but that was not the only problem.

title: Works great for the price.
text: I use this device only for the basic activity/fitness tracking and for the vibrating alarm to wake me up early on weekdays, but for that it works great. Paired to iPhone 13 Pro and it works with no issues. Simple, clear display. Wearable in the shower (unlike my old Fitbit). Tells me when I've sat for too long, default displays local weather, tracks HR and steps, all for under $35. Started using it on Sunday afternoon and now on Wednesday morning it still has 82% charge. I don't normally write reviews, but this device works so well for the cost I decided to rate it.

title: The Band Snap needs to be more secure
text: The band itself does not stay on. I lost it because it unsnapped

title: Works for me!
text: I bought this tracker to encourage me to increase my steps.  It works fine for that.  I like that my text messages show up on the watch which can be useful when I'm working out and don't have my phone within reach.  It has many features that I don't really use, but I can see how they can be useful.  I like that the band is not very big and that there is no metal clasp to trigger my nickel allergy.  The screen is a bit tiny so sometimes it's a bit hard for me to read it.  It's nice that it's waterproof.  The Mi Fit app takes some time to figure out.  I still have not figured out how to get the app to sync with the Health app on my iPhone.  Overall though, I'm happy with this tracker.  I ended up buying a second one for my teenage son.

title: Awesome Functionalities and Long Battery Life
text: This is one of my best purchases. The band is small and light with a beautiful screen. It tracks my my sleep cycles, heart rates, and stress levels. Long battery life too! Even with all these functionalities, it only uses about 10% battery a day. Great value!

title: Salt water kills it...completely dead
text:
Visited the beach and it started buzzing and is now dead. It has a swimming option and I've been to the pool with it so only guess is it's salt water.

No redress from company

title: IOS integration is horrible.
text: The app is terrible.  It cannot sync data with other apps even Apple health after the recent IOS update.

title: Better than I thought
text:
After seeing this product In a news article on Google news I decided to get one on the premise of how bad can it be for $30. I was morbidly curious. I picked one up and after getting it and charging it (it was as dead as a door nail) I got it all set up. The app is pretty good and navigating it is easy. Some things took some digging to find but nothing to crazy.

The watch itself has a lot of features and the display is very nice. The band even though it's just shaped rubber is nice and sturdy. I work with my hands all day and rarely catch the watch enough to unsnap it. All the tracking features works as you would expect. They are accurate and informative enough to be useful. My ONLY gripe with this watch is setting a custom do not disturb setting is a nightmare. It can be fixed so I'm not worried.

Overall I'm extremely impressed. I went into it thinking I'd charge it once and not carry after that but it's a great little piece of tech.

title: No so much
text:
I love this so much. Everything works as it should. For the price I'm pretty surprised. Got it as a gift and I love it. Love how you can change the watch faces. I found a Rick and Morty one!

Updating my review and down two stars. The battery stopped lasting longer than 3/4 days. I can no longer customize the watch faces. I could never use my own photos either. Days it's an option, but doesn't work.

title: Great watch, great price
text:
This activity watch is 100% worth it for the price and the features it has.

Now it's not as fancy as the other smart watch options out there but it does the job.

Pros:
Battery life is great
Lots of smart watch options: sleep tracking, pedometer, heart rate, phone notifications, workout tracking.
Accuracy of pedometer is pretty spot on.

Cons:
The accuracy of the calorie counter is off.
The accuracy of the sleep tracker is off.
No built in GPS- but can sync with your phones GPS.

If you want a watch to help you keep active, count steps, help log activity or push you to be more active, this does the trick.

I would highly recommend.

title: Has been unbeatable for the price and feature set
text:
It's insane how cheap this is for how much it competes against the far more expensive trackers like Fitbit and Garmin. The battery generally lasts me over a full week on a single charge even with the heartrate sensor checking every minute in the settings. Tracking has been very consistent and generally accurate for what I need, and the interface is clean and simple to navigate combined with a screen that can get extremely bright if you want it to.

My only real complaints are with the mobile app. At least on iOS, the app can be a little slow and needlessly convoluted to navigate. The watch has a lot of settings you can adjust through the app to make it work just how you need it, including setting multiple alarms and time ranges for when the lift-to-wake function activates (which you should probably do unless you want this thing waking you up in the middle of the night), but you have to open a whole lot of menus just to get to them. I also just wish some of the information was

title: A terrible product & an equally terrible return policy
text: After the initial charge, it worked fine. However when I attempted to charge it a second time, it would not take a charge even after keeping it plugged in overnight. To make matters even worse, when I attempted to return it, I discovered they penalize you 50% of the cost if anything is missing, even the box it came in! Since I tossed out the box, I'm screwed  half the cost. Does a cardboard box really cost over $20? I seriously doubt it!

title: Finally a watch that the battery lasts!
text:
Ok I've only had it for 3 days, but unlike the past 2 watches I purchased this one is still at 94%,the mobile app Is a bit annoying but the watch works great!
Now I've had it for over a week and at 74% yessss love this watch

title: Excellent battery power
text:
The best part of the device is the charge capacity. It lasts for about a week, while my FitBit had to be charged every day. The band is comfortable, and the steps and heartrate are accurate. The downside is some difficulty in use. It's hard to switch modes and I never figured out how to reset the time for Daylight Savings. The instruction booklet is not great; the translation to English should be better.
If you're looking for a basic tracker device with a great battery, this one is cheaper and better than FitBit. If you don't mind charging it every day, you might want to choose a FitBit.

title: Junk
text: Not even a full year old and the entire screen just randomly popped completely off from the tracker as I'm sitting here doing nothing. I don't wear it 24/7. I don't wear it in the shower, and I'm not doing any extremely strenuous activities. I only really walk with it. The app is terrible and the tracker clearly has quality issues.

title: The GPS disctance tracking is flaky. Otherwise great watch
text: Bought this for my 13 year old son. Many features, about 90 % of what my Apple Watch offers for ~ 10 X less money. The only worth mentioning issue is that the GPS tracking is not accurate at all. Huge discrepancies in the distance of up to a factor 2 (!) compared to my very accurate Apple GPS watch. Looking at the tracked map, there are big jumps, so this is not working well. People on forums also complain about this. There is no clear solution I think. Otherwise the pedometer/step counter based distance estimation without GPS is quite accurate and normally within 10% of what my GPS based Apple watch measures.  Overall, a very nice fitness watch for the money !

title: NOT comparable to other trackers
text:
I've had this tracking band for less than four hours and I already hate it. The tiny but thick user manual is next to worthless - it tells how to 'install' and 'uninstall' the band, yet the band comes already installed (assembled). There are only a few pages of basic 'get started' info, repeated in dozens of languages (which makes the manual so thick).

The Mi Fit app is only a little better. On the surface, it seems to be user-friendly. However, it really only lets you set the basic functions. Good luck if you want to know more about each function.

The band itself - I'll give it points for the variety of displays available. Lots to choose from and easy to change from one to another. And they look good! But nothing happens automatically, starting with waking the display. I should be able to turn my wrist and get immediate response. Nope. I have to touch the display in just the right place to activate. If I want it to tell me my heart rate, I have to tell

title: Toy-like sensor accuracy ruins an otherwise feature rich watch for the price.
text: This is a surprisingly feature rich fitness watch that doesn't break the bank, however, two huge cons made me ultimately return it. First, the Mi app is absolute garbage. It's incredibly intrusive and buggy. I used the Notify and Fitness app which was a huge improvement - and recommend you do the same. Sadly, the toy-like accuracy of the various sensors made me return it. It would frequently spike 30-40 beats high or low for no apparent reason and the sleep tracking was rarely accurate. A "get what you pay for" situation in terms of accuracy. Otherwise it would be a great watch, even with the Mi app annoyances.

title: Almost perfect!
text:
This smartwatch is superb in almost ever way. the display is fantastic. Good battery life. looks nice. feels fine on the wrist. Hundreds of watch faces available through good 3rd party apps in the Google app store. Watch's app software is nice and works as it should, also on Google app store.

I like the idea of the watch strap clasp, but it has already "pulled out" on me. I am hoping that a 3rd party will come along with a standard buckle type. Over 24,000 Amazon Mi-4 reviews out there, any entrepreneurs interested? Two months in and already over 3,000 Mi-5 reviews too. This is my only gripe with this smartwatch, and not worth removing a star over it.

I do not use all the bells and whistles, as in sports related fitness stuff, but I assume they work as does the heart rate monitor and notifications. I would not put my life on the line from any watch gadget anyway. Accuracy is not something I would be concerned with in a watch of this price, ball-park results is all

title: Sleep tracking is as good as the precious model
text:
I have been a Mi band user since the second model came out and once this newest one is out I ordered immediately. The upgrade on this 5th gen is huge.
Pros
The design is even better, the screen is hi-res, the way of charging is improved, the band is used with similar but slightly better texture material.
Cons
my biggest complaint is that sleep tracking isn't working properly, it often can't detect that I was sleeping and I never had an issue with any of the previous models.
I like the minimal and low-profile style. I'm not a big fan of a color screen with a tiny size, and there's no customization I can change that.

title: I love this thing
text: It has gotten me up out of mt seat and moving so that I meet my goals for the day. It is so light you can barely tell it is there. It connects thru the mobile app quickly and solidly and shows you a ton of data about your heart rate over time and your steps taken. It uses all this data and information that you input to calculate how many calories you've burned that day. Plus it keeps track of your sleep and shows you at what times you were in deep sleep or restorative REM sleep. I can't say personally how accurate any of it is, but I did take the time to watch several Youtube reviews about the Mi Band 5 before I pulled the trigger and they seem to show it to be actually quite accurate. All I know is that I could probably buy 4-6 of these for the price of one FitBit. You can't beat the price of this fitness band. And I keep hearing good things about Xiaomi's products. So far I have this band and their Basic 2 wireless earbuds and I have been impressed by both items and use them daily. S

title: I liked it
text: You will lose it because the strap/band its not very resistant to normal use.

title: Good for notifications
text: I've had this over a year and it's still working fine.  I set a daily alarm which vibrates to alert.  I get my phone notifications that I can choose which ones to send to the band.  Also I like that I can change the watch faces to different images.  I bought different bands than the original band because I was concerned about the band coming unlatched.  The bands I have are elastic and no way to accidentally come off.

title: No Explanations
text: While I will admit I am not the savviest when it comes to new tech stuff, there are really no instructions on how this product works or what exactly it does. I have no idea how to do or use the product other than the most basic things. There should be an explanation for each of the features and benefits. At this point, for me, it is an expensive step counter.

title: Love the watch, hate the band
text: I think the watch is cool. I love the face options. What I don't like is the watch band. My watch has come off a couple times to where I had to look for it. It's come off other times, too.

title: Good value
text:
I only needed a reliable pedometer but also something to track my cycling. So my requirements are but 2.
After some research I reluctantly purchased this product though I admit it does the job well and at a good price.

I did give this device the acid test for walking; and twice at that. I walked for about 1 km, then I took a bus for 20 minutes (no it did not count the bus distance), then walked for about another km. Then I went to a very crowded underground mall for about 40 minutes then took the metro back and finished another 1 km home. So lots of stop and go and the device actually did well.

Aside from the acid tests (on normal walks), I will say that at 1 in 15 I found instances that unexplained reason the device would detect me stopped (when I was not). The band would vibrate to alert me that I stopped and would do this repeatedly. However, the issue was not often--most of the time it does work well.

My biggest gripe and this may be my own de

title: I had to send it back for a refund!
text: I had to send it back because the blood pressure screen would go black. I got a replacement and it did the same samething.  I just sent it back and got a refund.

title: Great product
text: My second one my last one I had for over 5 years but lost it! This one is even better.

title: Best balance between tracker and smart watch
text: I've used a number of different smart watches, from expensive to cheap, but this one is my consistent favorite. It is reasonably accurate (less so when it comes to sleep tracking, but then I've not found any that do seem to accurately track my sleep) at activity tracking. I also make regular use of notifications on smart watches/trackers and this one supports all the notifications I need (including Gmail, which some others don't), and gives me enough of a message preview to know what kind of message has come in and what it is about. Battery life is fantastic, at upwards of 2 weeks with full use. Screen is bright and clear and the interface is nicely customizable with a large number of optional faces, including some you can customize. Light weight and comfortable, too.

title: Works well for the price
text: I like how easy it was to setup. The app isn't great but it works. Nicely integrates with sms notification and sends steps to Google fit. Don't rely on heart rate or sleep tracker. Good for a step tracking and watch notifications

title: Great fitness band... But...
text: This is a great fitness band as long as you aren't buying it for the sleep tracking. The sleep tracking didn't work at all even after trying multiple suggestions to fix it. That said, everything else is well done. I would have not returned it had the sleep tracking not been one of the main reasons I wanted it.

title: TERRIBLE CUSTOMER SERVICE. NONE!
text:
NEw update on previous write up. AVOID LIKE A PLAGUE.
IT failed no reason at all. Just stopped.
Contacted their 833 # and might as well have called no one. Just 2 stepped the issue entirely.

I am using a Letscom. Superior in all ways. SAVE YOUSELVES THIS DANCE. It’s frustrating when the mfg dances on its quality.

title: not for swimming
text:
doesnt really work for swimming.
the stop watch kept on turning on and off or pausing as it made contact with the water.

title: Works great
text: Does everything I want for a fraction of the cost of other smart watches

title: Worst App
text: This watch its self caused a burn mark on my wrist in the shape of the loop the band goes through to latch.  The app has lost my information twice and has made it so I have needed to get another account very disappointed..

title: Accuracy could be better
text: Nice fit, very comfortable, however I wish the accuracy was better, especially in regards to the pedometer.

title: Works ok but very firm
text: It's ok but if you have a small wrist like me it's not particularly comfortable

title: DEFECTIVE BATTERY
text:
I've now purchased two of these units and the battery has been defective on both of them. After 1-2 hours, the battery dies and the device is unusable.

Warning that this vendor will charge you to return the item, even if their quality and control process is to blame.

title: Excelente
text: Muy bueno y preciso con las notificaciones de app todo bien la carga dura mucho

title: Great step tracker at a great price
text: I have friends with $100+ fitness trackers or apple watches, and this $30 band does everything those other trackers do.  I've had it for 6 months, and am very happy with it.  The mobile app is really easy to use.  I tested it with another tracker and a walking map, and it seems very accurate.  I read in some reviews that the band can break after a few months, so I bought a pack of 12 bands in all colors.  I rotate between 3, and all are still in good shape.  Easy to pop in and out of the different bands.

title: Poor step counting
text: I got the Xiaomi Mi Band 5 solely to count my steps.  I have a fitbit and my smartphone also count.  But I don't want to carry my phone while walking, and the fitbit frequently falls out of its band.  The Mi Band 5 was recommended by consumer  reports and rated well.  HOWEVER, where the fitbit counted 12,000 steps and the smartphone (which I didn't always carry) counted 10,000, the xiaomi counted 6,000 steps.  When useing all three together and personally counting steps, the first two were roughly equal, and xiaomi was way less.  I wouldn't recommend the  Xiaomi Mi Band 5 for counting steps.

title: Great!
text: Much better than the fourth generation, cost-effective, long battery life, can detect heart rate, pressure, and sleep, including REM, except for taking a bath, basically every day

title: HR inaccurate for me
text: After using the watch a couple days a really enjoyed some a lot of the features and thought I was going to keep it. But unfortunately it was not able to measure my heart rate accurately while cycling. While cycling 30 miles a climbing 1000 feet of elevation it constantly measured my HR less than 100 bpm, much lower than compared to my chest strap wahoo tickr HR monitor. Even after trying multiple strap tightness settings (looser and tighter), different wrist positions positions and both wrists, I could not get an accurate HR reading. I was really hoping it would be more accurate for me but it simply did not work well on my wrist.

title: Lasted 10 months
text: Worked like a charm up until now. Not even sure how this happened or when, but this morning the screen popped out. It is still attached and still works, but there is no way to put it back in. I suspect it was held in place with adhesive. I can't really give it a super low rating, because it worked great while I did have it. I work in a fairly active environment, and to say this thing takes a beating would be an understatement. It only shows a couple visible scratches after all this time, and even with heart rate monitoring on, it lasts an average of a couple weeks for me (though I did disable the turn on screen feature when lifting your wrist.) All in all, it was a great device. Can't justify getting another one of these, when the latest model is available. Take what you will from this, but my final conclusion is that this is still a good tracker, despite my issue, it still technically works.

title: Perfect for anyone that has a reaction to other fitness trackers
text:
I bought this fitness tracker on a whim, hoping to find one that didn't irritate my skin. I've used other branded fitness trackers, some of them costing up to $200 that would all cause varying levels of rashes, pain and skin abrasion. Turns out I'm allergic to the nickel in the construction of the trackers.

This is the first fitness tracker that I've been able to wear for extended periods of time (more than two days) without having any reaction to it. I think the longest I've worn this tracker was a full week and only took it off for showering or to recharge the unit.

The app that is used with the tracker is good. It has a lot of information and options in it though it doesnt allow for entering in some body metrics that I would like (you can enter in your weight but you cant enter in body fat percentage unless you have the same brand scale).

The only down side I would say is that the display font is a bit small. I can read the time and steps taken

title: Perfect for wife
text: I bought this for my wife because her Fitbit broke. She didn't want to get a third Fitbit because of the price. She absolutely loves this watch. She says it works great, steps are more accurate than fitbit, (which makes her work our and walk more). She is already seeing a difference in her weight loss now. The fitbit tracks arm movements as steps where this actually tracks your whole body movements.

title: Had to return Almost immediately.
text:
Absolutely would not hold a charge. Had it on the charger for over 12 hours and wouldn’t go past 99%. Within 10 minutes of having it off the charger, it went from 99% to 84%.
Wore it to the gym and battery died within two hours of having it on with all battery draining options turned off.
Returned it almost immediately after getting it.

title: Such a deal
text: I got this on sale. It arrived sooner than expected. I've been wearing it since I got it. I was amazed how much I walk around the house. I walked indoors on the first day and reached my goal. I walk about 2,000 steps in the house, then go out and walk until I reach at least 8000 steps, but I've been exceeding that amount already since getting this.

title: Excelente producto
text: Muy buen diseño, fácil de manejar

title: Over a year strong
text: Ive had this over a year and it does everything i need, it helps manage my health, and it tells me if u get a call or a text.

title: Great for the price
text: I love this smart band for the price you can’t go wrong it works great for me

title: Sleep tracking not as accurate as advertised
text: Doesn't track naps accurately. Switching back to Huawei/Honor bands.

title: Not very good, had issues for active users.
text: The display is very hard to read outdoors if there’s any sunshine. The strap is not very secure when doing sport, because you have to press the small post into a hole in the band that is too small. The heart rate seems accurate though, but the transition from one screen to another is not smooth & the numbers & letters are too small to read easily when moving.

title: Nice upgrade
text:
I have used the band 4 for a few months and I keep them on all the time. I have gotten used to them on my hands all the time. They’re incredibly affordable for their features, and I have built a solid trust for this company. Which is why I upgraded to the 5 for my dad. Upgrade includes a nicer more visible screen. New features like PAI score and stress levels. And most importantly, you can charge with a magnetic clip that attaches from the bottom.
I have built a strong trust for Xaomi from these products

title: Too big
text: Again I had to send a fitness tracker because I have such s small wrist that it felt too big on my wrist.  The band went around fine it was the size of the watch itself that is too big.

title: Great gift. Great smartwatch/fitness tracker on a budget
text:
For the price this is a great product:
-Build: the strap is made of silicone I think and feels lightweight and comfortable on the wrist.
-Screen: the screen is big enough for daily use. It's not high resolution but it's enough to read notifications and menu items and the time at a glance.
-Software: the MiFit app is okay. Don't expect perfect software but it is okay for what it does. My needs aren't high for the band in the first place and it fulfills them all.
-It's easy to remove the Mi Band 5 from the watch band for cleaning.
-The magnetic charger works well and it charges fairly quickly from a wall outlet.
-Battery life has been excellent. I charge it then forget it has a battery until a week or more later.

title: Works
text: Came in and work as advertised. Syncs to phone and heart rate works. Comfortable on the wrist. No problems so far having used it for a month.

title: Simple to use
text: Inexpensive watch, great features - you can receive your text messages, Facebook Messages, steps, time, heartbeat, gives feedback on your sleeping - the battery life is crazy amazing and it recharges very fast.  Love it!!!!

title: Great inexpensive tracking watch
text: I like how simple it is to set up. I only use for tracking steps, sleep, and the time. I do have it set up to get message alerts, which is great. I wanted an inexpensive tracking watch and this fits my needs.

title: Cheap, useful , and makes you wanna stay healthy.
text: Vary good little thing. I love this thing that its so cheap and yet so helpful. As a truck driver i noticed that i do more activities walking and generally staying outside for longer . It makes you want to start exercising more .  All tho its great i did found little flaw, steps count go up little while you drive , i guess it thinks you walk when you actually turn your steering wheel but im not sure.

title: Práctico, económico.
text: Práctico, económico. Buen artículo.

title: Buy it !!!
text: Great fitness watch for the money .... very comfortable, looks great , love the color screen and brightness and not too big

title: A Big Improvement over the Mi Band 4 Except for One Thing
text:
I got the Mi Band 5 as a replacement for my Mi Band 4, which I bought about 3 years ago. I was very happy with my Mi Band 4 because it was much cheaper than other fitness band on the market then and included only the functions I wanted. Best of all, I had to recharge it only once per month, even with daily use. I gave up on my Mi Band 4 only because eventually it wouldn't recharge the battery any higher than 30%.

So, how does the 5 measure up against the 4? Well, it’s a little more expensive but still incredibly inexpensive compared to other fitness trackers. It has more features than the 4 did, including PAI, stress monitoring and even reports on how long you nap during the day (if you do). The 5 also measures heart rate (pulse) and daily steps like my 4 did. The PAI (physiological activity indicator) does constant monitoring of your heart rate to calculate a PAI score. I haven’t used it or the stress monitoring because constant monitoring drains the battery faster.<

title: PURE 100% AWESOME
text: We love Xiaomi products. Mibox and this product just so well designed and we love all of them.  No problems

title: Excelente estado
text: Me llegó en muy buen estado, no presenta marcas de golpes o daños, al ser un regalo, aun no lo puedo abrir, pero espero por lo que veo en la caja, que el dispositivo se encuentre en igualdad de condiciones, en excelente estado y sin golpes.

title: Constant Companion
text:
Took a while to get here from overseas, but worth the wait. Paid for itself because of a work fitness program that requires a device to track exercise, sleep etc. With all features (some requiring constant Bluetooth connection) turned on, battery lasts just over a week.
Very happy with all aspects of the band, although a traditional belt clip pedometer seems to register more steps (I am constantly pushing and pulling objects at work which can blunt the impact on my wrist of taking steps).
Excellent for the price.
I'm going to skip buying the MiBand 6, (although the pulse oximeter and the larger edge-to-edge display are quite tempting), but will definitely upgrade to the 7 when it comes out.
This watchband works well for me, black stainless looks a bit classier than silicone: https://smile.amazon.com/OLLIVAN-Replacement-Accessories-Wristbands-Adjustable/dp/B07V45NKHN/ref=psdc_10048703011_t2_B08JD1BS87?th=1

title: Love this little thing!
text: I've had mine on for three solid days now, and have not had to charge it once, yet.  The many options for display are lovely and fun!  It was kinda hard to find them, but they are in the "store" section of the mobile app.  However, none of the displays cost anything.  Don't know why they call that "store." ??  But, it seems accurate with the heart rate and steps and I love how you can sync it with your phone health data and connect it via bluetooth.  Why pay $300 on something like this when you can pay $30???  It's also waterproof.  Love it.

title: Best Fitness Tracker!
text: I love this tracker.  It's fun to use.  I love that the heart rate and steps and time are on the face of the tracker.  The app that goes with the tracker is terrific.  It keeps track of how long you sleep which is so valuable.  I am so happy with this purchase and it is so affordable compared to other trackers.

title: Excellent watch
text: Very good for the price. recommend

title: For 35 bucks it's okay. The app truly sucks though...
text: SpO2 is missing from global version. App is horrible.

title: Great product worth the money
text: I absolutely love my watch I use it daily and has had it for a little over a year now this one I bought for my mom as a gift
